FBR Notice Guide

Don't panic! Here's what to do if you receive a notice from the Federal Board of Revenue.

Why did I get a notice?

Notices are often auto-generated by the FBR system. Common reasons include:

  • You bought or sold property/cars but are a non-filer.
  • Your bank reported large transactions, but you haven't filed a return.
  • You filed a return, but there is a mismatch between your declared income and your expenses (wealth statement).
  • You missed the filing deadline (typically Sept 30th).

Immediate Steps to Take

1

Check the deadline

Notices usually give you 15 to 30 days to respond. Note this date carefully to avoid further penalties.

2

Login to IRIS

Go to iris.fbr.gov.pk. Check your Inbox to read the official notice and see exactly which section of the law they are quoting (e.g., Section 114 for failure to file).

3

File Your Return

If the notice is for non-filing, the simplest solution is to file your return immediately! Use our Tax Calculator to prepare your numbers. Late filing carries a penalty of PKR 1,000 per month.

When to hire a professional

While you can reply to simple notices yourself through the IRIS portal, you should consult a Chartered Accountant or Tax Lawyer if:

  • The notice demands a large sum of tax or penalty.
  • The notice is for a tax audit (Section 177).
  • You have undeclared foreign assets.
  • You do not understand the legal jargon in the notice.
